---
title: "OptimalPhaseAnalyes"
author: "Fatih Serin"
date: "2026-04-27"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(ggplot2)
theme_set(theme_bw(base_size = 13, base_family = "Arial") +
            theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 15),
                  axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
                  plot.title = element_text(size = 16)))
options(
  ggplot2.discrete.colour = function(...) scale_colour_grey(...),
  ggplot2.discrete.fill   = function(...) scale_fill_grey(...),
  ggplot2.continuous.colour = function(...) scale_colour_gradient(low = "grey80", high = "black", ...),
  ggplot2.continuous.fill   = function(...) scale_fill_gradient(low = "grey80", high = "black", ...)
)
```

```{r Load data and perform chance-level rejection analysis, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(car)
library(lme4)
library(emmeans)
library(poolr)
library(BayesFactor)
library(ggeffects)
library(here)

# Load anonymised data
load("Data.RData")

# Set the seed for reproducibility (affects all permutation / sampling below)
set.seed(1996)


# Participant Exclusion w/ Permutation ----------------------------------------
PIDs <- unique(df_retrieval$ParticipantID)

Feedbacks    <- data.frame()
Exp4Data     <- data.frame()
nIterations  <- 10000
Includedlist <- vector()
IncludedPIDs <- vector()
ExcludedPIDs <- vector()

for (iSub in 1:length(PIDs)) {

  Excluded <- 0
  NullDist <- data.frame(Acc = rep(NA, nIterations))

  df_Sub_ret  <- df_retrieval[df_retrieval$ParticipantID == PIDs[iSub], ]
  df_Sub_enc  <- df_encoding[df_encoding$ParticipantID   == PIDs[iSub], ]
  df_Sub_surv <- df_survey[df_survey$ParticipantID       == PIDs[iSub], ]

  for (i in 1:nIterations) {
    Responses <- df_Sub_ret$response
    Answers   <- sample(Responses)
    NullDist[i, 1] <- (sum(Responses == Answers, na.rm = TRUE) / length(Responses)) * 100
  }

  # One-sided 95th percentile of the null = significance threshold (p < .05)
  Significance_Threshold <- quantile(NullDist$Acc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)

  # print(ggplot(NullDist, aes(x = Acc)) + geom_density(fill = "gray") +
  #         geom_vline(xintercept = mean(df_Sub_ret$Accuracy, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
  #                    color = "green", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  #         geom_vline(xintercept = Significance_Threshold,
  #                    color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  #         ggtitle(paste("Sub -", PIDs[iSub])))

  df_Sub_sum <- df_Sub_ret |>
    group_by(Condition) |>
    summarise(
      PropCorrect = mean(Accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
      n           = sum(!is.na(Accuracy)),
      .groups     = "drop"
    )

  MissingEncoding  <- sum(is.na(df_Sub_enc$response))
  MissingRetrieval <- sum(is.na(df_Sub_ret$response))

  print(paste("ID = ", PIDs[iSub], ' missing responses. Encoding: ', MissingEncoding,
              ' Retrieval: ', MissingRetrieval))

  # ── Exclusion criteria ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  if (mean(df_Sub_ret$Accuracy, na.rm = TRUE) * 100 <= Significance_Threshold) {
    # Accuracy not above chance
    print(paste("ID = ", PIDs[iSub], ' is at chance-level!'))
    Excluded <- 1
    ExcludedPIDs <- c(ExcludedPIDs, PIDs[iSub])
  } else if (PIDs[iSub] == "267") {
    # Participant admitted to not paying attention
    Excluded <- 1
    ExcludedPIDs <- c(ExcludedPIDs, PIDs[iSub])
  } else if (min(df_Sub_sum$n) < 6) {
    # At least one condition has fewer than half its trials
    print(paste("Lowest trial N per condition: ", min(df_Sub_sum$n)))
    Excluded <- 1
    ExcludedPIDs <- c(ExcludedPIDs, PIDs[iSub])
  } else {
    # Included: record condition assignment and PID in parallel (one entry each;
    # assumes exactly one ConditionID per participant).
    Includedlist <- c(Includedlist, as.numeric(unique(df_Sub_ret$ConditionID)))
    IncludedPIDs <- c(IncludedPIDs, PIDs[iSub])
  }

  # ── Survey / free-text feedback ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  Strategy      <- as.vector(na.omit(unique(df_Sub_surv$response.Strategy)))
  StrategyFreq  <- as.vector(na.omit(unique(df_Sub_surv$response.StrategyFreq)))

  # Take the 2nd timing response if more than one was given, else the single one.
  TimingResponses <- as.vector(na.omit(unique(df_Sub_surv$response.StrategyTiming)))
  if (length(TimingResponses) > 1) {
    StrategyTiming <- toString(TimingResponses[[2]])
  } else {
    StrategyTiming <- toString(TimingResponses)
  }

  OtherStrategy <- as.vector(na.omit(unique(df_Sub_surv$response.OtherStrategy)))
  FreeFeedback  <- as.vector(na.omit(unique(df_Sub_surv$response.FreeFeedback)))
  Seriousness   <- as.vector(na.omit(unique(df_Sub_surv$response.Seriousness)))
  ConditionID   <- unique(df_Sub_ret$ConditionID)
  SubID         <- iSub

  if (is_empty(Strategy))       Strategy       <- NA
  if (is_empty(StrategyFreq))   StrategyFreq   <- NA
  if (is_empty(OtherStrategy))  OtherStrategy  <- NA
  if (is_empty(FreeFeedback))   FreeFeedback   <- NA
  if (is_empty(StrategyTiming)) StrategyTiming <- NA
  if (is_empty(Seriousness))    Seriousness    <- NA

  Feedback <- data.frame(SubID, ConditionID, Excluded, Strategy, StrategyFreq,
                         StrategyTiming, OtherStrategy, FreeFeedback, Seriousness)

  Feedbacks <- rbind(Feedbacks, Feedback)
}

missing_IDs <- setdiff(1:384, sort(as.numeric(Includedlist)))
paste(missing_IDs, collapse = ', ')

cat("Exclusion Rate: ", ((length(PIDs) - length(IncludedPIDs)) / length(PIDs)) * 100,
    "%. ", (length(PIDs) - length(IncludedPIDs)), "out of ", length(PIDs))

# Remove duplicate ConditionIDs, keeping the first instance of each.
dups      <- which(duplicated(Includedlist))
FinalList <- if (length(dups)) IncludedPIDs[-dups] else IncludedPIDs

Exp4Data <- df_retrieval[df_retrieval$ParticipantID %in% FinalList, ]

IncludeColumns <- c("rt", "response", "ConditionID", "TrialType", "ClipID",
                    "BlockNum", "Condition", "SoundCategory", "CorrectKey",
                    "Accuracy")

Exp4Data <- Exp4Data[IncludeColumns]

# Use ConditionID as the analysis-level ParticipantID
names(Exp4Data)[names(Exp4Data) == 'ConditionID'] <- 'ParticipantID'

write.csv(Exp4Data, here("Exp4Data.csv"), row.names = FALSE)

# Sample characteristics: age. The Age question is the first survey-text item
# (trial_type == "survey-text", trial_index == 1). Note: response is stored as
# literal text, e.g. response = 'list(Age = "23")' (a character string, not an
# actual R list), so we pull the quoted value out with a regex rather than
# list-indexing it.
Age <- df_survey |>
  filter(trial_type == "survey-text", trial_index == 1) |>
  mutate(Age = as.numeric(sub('.*Age\\s*=\\s*"([^"]*)".*', "\\1", response))) |>
  filter(ParticipantID %in% FinalList) |>
  group_by(ParticipantID) |>
  summarise(Age = dplyr::first(Age, na_rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") |>
  pull(Age)

cat("Age: M =", round(mean(Age, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    ", SD =", round(sd(Age, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    ", range =", paste(range(Age, na.rm = TRUE), collapse = "-"), "\n")

```


```{r Confirmatory Analysis}

library(tidyverse)
library(car)
library(lme4)
library(emmeans)
library(poolr)
library(BayesFactor)
library(ggeffects)
library(ggplot2)
library(here)
library(devEMF)

theme_set(theme_bw(base_size = 13, base_family = "Arial") +
            theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 15),
                  axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
                  plot.title = element_text(size = 16)))
options(
  ggplot2.discrete.colour = function(...) scale_colour_grey(...),
  ggplot2.discrete.fill   = function(...) scale_fill_grey(...),
  ggplot2.continuous.colour = function(...) scale_colour_gradient(low = "grey80", high = "black", ...),
  ggplot2.continuous.fill   = function(...) scale_fill_gradient(low = "grey80", high = "black", ...)
)

Exp4Data <- read.csv(here("Exp4Data.csv"))

# Per-condition, per-participant accuracy (Accuracy still logical here → mean OK)
Exp4Sum <- Exp4Data |>
  group_by(Condition, ParticipantID) |>
  summarise(
    PropCorrect = mean(Accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
    n           = n(),
    .groups     = "drop"
  )

Exp4Sum$Condition     <- as.numeric(Exp4Sum$Condition)
Exp4Sum$Condition_rad <- Exp4Sum$Condition * (pi / 180)

# Accuracy across blocks (for the learning-curve plot)
BlockSummary <- Exp4Data |>
  group_by(ParticipantID, BlockNum) |>
  summarise(
    PropCorrect = mean(Accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
    n           = n(),
    .groups     = "drop"
  )

# Overall accuracy per participant (pooled over all trials)
ParticipantSummary <- Exp4Data |>
  group_by(ParticipantID) |>
  summarise(
    PropCorrect = mean(Accuracy, na.rm = TRUE),
    n           = n(),
    .groups     = "drop"
  )

cat("Overall accuracy: M =", round(mean(ParticipantSummary$PropCorrect, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    ", SD =", round(sd(ParticipantSummary$PropCorrect, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")

# Recode logical Accuracy (TRUE/FALSE) to 1/0, then to a factor for glm/glmer.
Exp4Data$Accuracy[Exp4Data$Accuracy == TRUE]  <- 1
Exp4Data$Accuracy[Exp4Data$Accuracy == FALSE] <- 0
Exp4Data$Accuracy <- as.factor(Exp4Data$Accuracy)

# Keep Condition in DEGREES (matches Exp4Sum) and add an explicit RADIAN column
# for the circular/sinusoidal analyses.
Exp4Data$Condition     <- as.numeric(Exp4Data$Condition)
Exp4Data$Condition_rad <- Exp4Data$Condition * (pi / 180)

# Learning curve across blocks
p <- ggplot(BlockSummary, aes(x = BlockNum, y = PropCorrect,
                              group = ParticipantID)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.4, colour = "grey50") +
  geom_point(size = 1.8, alpha = 0.4, colour = "grey50") +
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = mean, geom = "line",
               colour = "black", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.25, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = unique(BlockSummary$BlockNum)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title    = "Retrieval Accuracy Across Blocks",
    subtitle = length(unique(Exp4Data$ParticipantID)),
    x        = "Block Number",
    y        = "Proportion Correct"
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

print(p)

# X-axis breaks/labels shared by both "accuracy across phase lags" figures
# below: only these 7 phase-lag values get a labelled tick (no trailing ".0"),
# and vertical gridlines appear only at these positions (no minor gridlines).
PhaseLagBreaks <- c(-135, -90, -45, 0, 45, 90, 135)
PhaseLagLabels <- as.character(PhaseLagBreaks)

# ── Plot: accuracy across conditions (individual participants) ───────────────
p_conditions <- ggplot(Exp4Sum, aes(x = Condition, y = PropCorrect,
                         group = ParticipantID, colour = ParticipantID)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.6, alpha = 0.4, colour = "grey50") +
  geom_point(size = 1.8, alpha = 0.4, colour = "grey50") +
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = mean, geom = "line",
               colour = "black", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.25, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = PhaseLagBreaks, labels = PhaseLagLabels) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  labs(
    title    = "Memory Accuracy Across Phase Lags (chance level = 25%)",
    x        = "Phase Lags (degrees)",
    y        = "Proportion Correct"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13, base_family = "Arial") +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text = element_text(size = 15), axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16),
        panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank())

print(p_conditions)

# ── Plot: accuracy across conditions (mean +/- 95% CI shaded band) ───────────
# Same underlying data as p_conditions, but summarised across participants per
# condition and shown as a mean line with a shaded 95% CI ribbon instead of
# individual participant lines.
Exp4CondSummary <- Exp4Sum |>
  group_by(Condition) |>
  summarise(
    Mean = mean(PropCorrect, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD   = sd(PropCorrect, na.rm = TRUE),
    n    = sum(!is.na(PropCorrect)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) |>
  mutate(
    SE       = SD / sqrt(n),
    CI_lower = Mean - qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * SE,
    CI_upper = Mean + qt(0.975, df = n - 1) * SE
  )

p_conditions_ci <- ggplot(Exp4CondSummary, aes(x = Condition, y = Mean)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = CI_lower, ymax = CI_upper), fill = "grey70", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_line(colour = "black", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(colour = "black", size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.25, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = PhaseLagBreaks, labels = PhaseLagLabels) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = sort(unique(c(seq(0.2, 0.55, by = 0.1), 0.25, 0.55))),
    labels = scales::percent_format()
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(0.2, 0.55)) +
  labs(
    title    = "Memory Accuracy Across Phase Lags",
    x        = "Phase Lags (degrees)",
    y        = "Proportion Correct"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13, base_family = "Arial") +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        axis.text = element_text(size = 15), axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16),
        panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank())

print(p_conditions_ci)

# ── Per-subject sinusoidal logistic regression (test of phase modulation) ──────
ParticipantIDs <- as.character(sort(as.numeric(unique(Exp4Data$ParticipantID))))
n_subj         <- length(ParticipantIDs)

logregress_ps   <- numeric(n_subj)
logregress_Fs   <- numeric(n_subj)

A_obs       <- numeric(n_subj)
A_floor     <- numeric(n_subj)
A_corrected <- numeric(n_subj)

nIterations <- 1000 # permutation iterations for the amplitude null floor

for (iSub in 1:length(ParticipantIDs)) {

  subj_data <- Exp4Data[Exp4Data$ParticipantID == ParticipantIDs[iSub], ]
  subj_data <- subj_data[!is.na(subj_data$Accuracy), ]

  df <- data.frame(
    Accuracy = as.numeric(as.character(subj_data$Accuracy)),
    pred_sin = sin(subj_data$Condition_rad),   # phase in radians
    pred_cos = cos(subj_data$Condition_rad)
  )

  g1 <- glm(Accuracy ~ pred_sin + pred_cos, data = df, family = binomial(link = "logit"))

  # Wald F-test on the two sinusoidal terms (matches MATLAB's coefTest)
  wald_result         <- linearHypothesis(g1, c("pred_sin = 0", "pred_cos = 0"), test = "F")
  logregress_Fs[iSub] <- wald_result[2, "F"]
  logregress_ps[iSub] <- wald_result[2, "Pr(>F)"]

  # Observed modulation amplitude
  b <- coef(g1)
  A_obs[iSub] <- sqrt(b["pred_sin"]^2 + b["pred_cos"]^2)

  # Permutation null floor: shuffle phase labels, keep Accuracy fixed
  A_null <- numeric(nIterations)
  for (iPerm in 1:nIterations) {
    idx <- sample(nrow(df))
    gb  <- suppressWarnings(glm(df$Accuracy ~ df$pred_sin[idx] + df$pred_cos[idx],
                                family = binomial(link = "logit")))
    cb  <- coef(gb)
    A_null[iPerm] <- sqrt(cb[2]^2 + cb[3]^2)
  }
  A_floor[iSub]     <- mean(A_null, na.rm = TRUE)

  A_corrected[iSub] <- A_obs[iSub] - A_floor[iSub]
}

# Combine evidence across subjects
p_pvals <- ggplot(data.frame(logregress_ps = logregress_ps), aes(x = logregress_ps)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 25, fill = "grey60", colour = "black") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of p-values",
    x     = "p-value",
    y     = "Count"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 15), axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16))

print(p_pvals)

# Histogram of the corrected phase-modulation effect size (A_corrected)
# A_corrected = observed sinusoidal amplitude minus the permutation null floor,
# i.e. how much bigger each participant's modulation is than chance alone.
p_effect <- ggplot(data.frame(A_corrected = A_corrected), aes(x = A_corrected)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 20, fill = "grey60", colour = "black") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey30") +
  labs(
    title    = "Distribution of Effect Sizes",
    x        = "Effect Size",
    y        = "Count"
  ) +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 15), axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16))

print(p_effect)

# Save the accuracy-across-conditions plot (95% CI shaded-band version, not
# the individual-participant-lines version), the p-value histogram, and the
# effect-size histogram as .emf files (soft-coded path via here::here())
ggsave(here("AccuracyAcrossConditions.emf"), plot = p_conditions_ci, width = 7, height = 5, device = devEMF::emf)
ggsave(here("PValueHistogram.emf"),          plot = p_pvals,         width = 7, height = 5, device = devEMF::emf)
ggsave(here("EffectSizeHistogram.emf"),      plot = p_effect,        width = 7, height = 5, device = devEMF::emf)


# One-sided Bayesian one-sample t-test: is the corrected amplitude above the null
# floor (d > 0)? bf[1] is the "0 < d < Inf" slice vs the point null; 1 / bf[1] is
# the evidence FOR the null against a positive effect (BF01).
bf <- ttestBF(A_corrected, nullInterval = c(0, Inf))
1 / bf[1]

# Report mean, SD, and Cohen's d (one-sample: mean / SD, standardised distance
# of the corrected effect size from zero) for the Bayesian t-test above.
cat("Effect size (A_corrected): M =", round(mean(A_corrected, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    ", SD =", round(sd(A_corrected, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    ", Cohen's d =", round(mean(A_corrected, na.rm = TRUE) / sd(A_corrected, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")

# Fisher's method: printing the poolr object shows the chi-square statistic, df and p
comb_sin <- fisher(logregress_ps)
cat("Sinusoidal phase effect (combined across people):\n"); print(comb_sin)
comb_p   <- comb_sin$p

#cat("Fixed-effects combined BF01:", combined_BF, "\n")
```


```{r Exploratory Analysis}

## Correlate overall memory performance with the phase-modulation amplitude
# Match by ID (not row position). Coerce ParticipantID to character on both
# sides so the merge keys have the same type.
ParticipantSummary$ParticipantID <- as.character(ParticipantSummary$ParticipantID)

plot_df <- merge(
  ParticipantSummary,
  data.frame(ParticipantID = ParticipantIDs,
             A_corrected   = A_corrected,
             stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
  by = "ParticipantID"
)

# remove zeros?
# plot_df <- plot_df[plot_df$A_corrected > 0, ]

plot_df$PropCorrect_z <- scale(plot_df$PropCorrect)[, 1]
plot_df$A_corrected_z <- scale(plot_df$A_corrected)[, 1]

# Correlation (r is scale-invariant, so raw and z give the same estimate)
cor.test(plot_df$PropCorrect, plot_df$A_corrected)

p_corr <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(PropCorrect_z, A_corrected_z)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, colour = "black") +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", colour = "black", fill = "grey70") +
  labs(
    title = "Overall Performance vs. Corrected Effect Size",
    x     = "Overall Accuracy (z-scored)",
    y     = "Effect Size (z-scored)"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 13, base_family = "Arial") +
  theme(axis.text = element_text(size = 15), axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16))

print(p_corr)

ggsave(here("PerformanceEffectSizeCorrelation.emf"), plot = p_corr, width = 7, height = 5, device = devEMF::emf)

## Robustness check: does the phase-modulation effect hold within just the
## higher-performing half of the sample? Median-split on overall accuracy,
## then re-run the main confirmatory test (one-sample Bayesian t-test on the
## permutation-corrected effect size) restricted to the upper-half performers.
## A_corrected is already computed per participant, independent of this split,
## so we simply subset it rather than re-running the per-subject regressions.
median_perf <- median(plot_df$PropCorrect, na.rm = TRUE)
A_corrected_high <- plot_df$A_corrected[plot_df$PropCorrect >= median_perf]
PropCorrect_high <- plot_df$PropCorrect[plot_df$PropCorrect >= median_perf]

cat("High performers (upper half on overall accuracy, median =", round(median_perf, 3),
    ", n =", length(A_corrected_high), "):\n")
cat("Overall accuracy (High performers only): M =", round(mean(PropCorrect_high, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    ", SD =", round(sd(PropCorrect_high, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")
cat("Effect size: M =", round(mean(A_corrected_high, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    ", SD =", round(sd(A_corrected_high, na.rm = TRUE), 3), "\n")

bf_high <- ttestBF(A_corrected_high, nullInterval = c(0, Inf))
cat("One-sample Bayesian t-test (High performers only), effect size > 0:\n")
print(bf_high)
cat("BF01 =", round(1 / extractBF(bf_high[1])$bf, 3), "\n")

# Fisher's combined test on the same high-performer subgroup, using each
# participant's per-subject Wald F-test p-value (logregress_ps, matched to
# ParticipantIDs by ID) from the confirmatory chunk.
ps_high <- logregress_ps[match(plot_df$ParticipantID[plot_df$PropCorrect >= median_perf], ParticipantIDs)]

comb_sin_high <- fisher(ps_high)
cat("Sinusoidal phase effect, Fisher's combined test (High performers only):\n")
print(comb_sin_high)
cat("chi-square =", round(comb_sin_high$statistic, 3),
    ", df =", attr(comb_sin_high$statistic, "df"),
    ", p =", round(comb_sin_high$p, 4), "\n")


## Factor model: is any specific phase lag overall different from the others?
# Exp4Data.model <- glmer(
#   Accuracy ~ as.factor(Condition) + (1 | ClipID) + (1 | ParticipantID),
#   data   = Exp4Data,
#   family = binomial
# )
# 
# Anova(Exp4Data.model)
# summary(Exp4Data.model)
# plot(predict_response(Exp4Data.model, terms = "Condition [all]"))
# pairs(emmeans(Exp4Data.model, ~ Condition))


Exp4Sum$Sine <- sin(Exp4Sum$Condition_rad)
Exp4Sum$Cosine <- cos(Exp4Sum$Condition_rad)

Exp4Sum$ParticipantID <- as.factor(Exp4Sum$ParticipantID)


Exp4Sum.BFNull <- lmBF(
  PropCorrect ~ ParticipantID,
  data = Exp4Sum,
  whichRandom = "ParticipantID"
)

Exp4Sum.BFFull <- lmBF(
  PropCorrect ~ Sine + Cosine + ParticipantID,
  data = Exp4Sum,
  whichRandom = "ParticipantID"
)


Exp4Sum.BFNull/Exp4Sum.BFFull


## Harmonics in a SINGLE per-person model (trial level) ───────────────────────
# One logistic model per person holding the 4, 8, 12 and 16 Hz harmonics of the
# fundamental as sin/cos pairs. Rather than testing each harmonic's pair one at
# a time, we run a SINGLE joint Wald F-test across all four harmonics (all 8
# sin/cos coefficients simultaneously, 8 df) to ask whether there is any
# harmonic structure at all in a participant's phase-tuning curve. On 16
# equally spaced phase bins these harmonics form an (approximately, under
# balanced trials) orthogonal Fourier basis.
# Harmonic h corresponds to h x 4 Hz (h = 1..4 -> 4, 8, 12, 16 Hz).
Hz_labels <- c("4Hz", "8Hz", "12Hz", "16Hz")

ps_harmonics_joint <- setNames(numeric(n_subj), ParticipantIDs)

for (iSub in 1:n_subj) {
  subj_data <- Exp4Data[Exp4Data$ParticipantID == ParticipantIDs[iSub], ]
  subj_data <- subj_data[!is.na(subj_data$Accuracy), ]

  th  <- subj_data$Condition_rad
  dat <- data.frame(
    Accuracy = as.numeric(as.character(subj_data$Accuracy)),
    s1 = sin(1 * th), c1 = cos(1 * th),   # 4 Hz (fundamental)
    s2 = sin(2 * th), c2 = cos(2 * th),   # 8 Hz
    s3 = sin(3 * th), c3 = cos(3 * th),   # 12 Hz
    s4 = sin(4 * th), c4 = cos(4 * th)    # 16 Hz
  )

  gfull <- suppressWarnings(glm(
    Accuracy ~ s1 + c1 + s2 + c2 + s3 + c3 + s4 + c4,
    data = dat, family = binomial(link = "logit")))

  # Joint Wald F-test on all four harmonics' sin/cos pairs at once (8 df)
  wald_joint <- linearHypothesis(
    gfull,
    c("s1 = 0", "c1 = 0", "s2 = 0", "c2 = 0", "s3 = 0", "c3 = 0", "s4 = 0", "c4 = 0"),
    test = "F"
  )
  ps_harmonics_joint[iSub] <- wald_joint[2, "Pr(>F)"]
}

# Combine the joint per-participant p-values across participants (Fisher's
# method); the printed poolr object shows the chi-square statistic, df and p
# for the combined test of "any harmonic structure, all frequencies at once"
comb_harmonics_joint <- fisher(ps_harmonics_joint[!is.na(ps_harmonics_joint)])
cat("Combined harmonic effect (4, 8, 12, 16 Hz tested jointly):\n")
print(comb_harmonics_joint)
comb_p_harmonics_joint <- comb_harmonics_joint$p


# ── Fitted phase tuning for participants with a significant Wald F-test ────────
# Self-contained loop: refit each significant participant's sinusoidal logistic
# model and plot the fitted tuning curves together, marking each peak angle.
# Fitted curve: P(correct | theta) = plogis(b0 + b_sin*sin(theta) + b_cos*cos(theta)).
# The sinusoid peaks where its derivative is zero, i.e. theta = atan2(b_sin, b_cos),
# which is the participant's preferred (peak) phase angle.
alpha_level <- 0.05
sig_idx     <- which(logregress_ps < alpha_level)   # from the confirmatory chunk

if (length(sig_idx) == 0) {
  message("No participants with p < ", alpha_level, " in the Wald F-test.")
} else {
  theta_grid <- seq(-pi, pi, length.out = 400)
  fit_long   <- data.frame()
  peak_pts   <- data.frame()

  for (j in sig_idx) {
    subj_data <- Exp4Data[Exp4Data$ParticipantID == ParticipantIDs[j], ]
    subj_data <- subj_data[!is.na(subj_data$Accuracy), ]

    df <- data.frame(
      Accuracy = as.numeric(as.character(subj_data$Accuracy)),
      pred_sin = sin(subj_data$Condition_rad),
      pred_cos = cos(subj_data$Condition_rad)
    )

    g1 <- glm(Accuracy ~ pred_sin + pred_cos, data = df,
              family = binomial(link = "logit"))
    b  <- coef(g1)                                       # intercept, sin, cos

    # Fitted curve over the full circle
    pfit <- plogis(b[1] + b[2] * sin(theta_grid) + b[3] * cos(theta_grid))
    fit_long <- rbind(fit_long, data.frame(
      ParticipantID = ParticipantIDs[j],
      theta_deg     = theta_grid * 180 / pi,
      pfit          = pfit
    ))

    # Peak (preferred) angle and its fitted accuracy
    theta_peak <- atan2(b[2], b[3])
    peak_pts <- rbind(peak_pts, data.frame(
      ParticipantID = ParticipantIDs[j],
      theta_deg     = theta_peak * 180 / pi,
      pfit          = plogis(b[1] + b[2] * sin(theta_peak) + b[3] * cos(theta_peak))
    ))
  }

  p_sig <- ggplot(fit_long, aes(theta_deg, pfit,
                                colour = ParticipantID, linetype = ParticipantID)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 0.9, alpha = 0.85) +
    geom_point(data = peak_pts, size = 3, show.legend = FALSE) +  # highlight peak angle
    geom_text(data = peak_pts,
              aes(label = paste0(round(theta_deg), "\u00b0")),
              vjust = -0.9, size = 3, show.legend = FALSE) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0.25, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
    scale_colour_grey(start = 0, end = 0.7) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-180, 180, 45), limits = c(-180, 180)) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
    labs(
      title    = paste0("Fitted phase tuning — significant participants (p < ",
                        alpha_level, ", n = ", length(sig_idx), ")"),
      subtitle = "Points mark each participant's peak (preferred) phase angle; dashed = chance",
      x        = "Phase angle (degrees)",
      y        = "Fitted P(correct)",
      colour   = "Participant", linetype = "Participant"
    )

  print(p_sig)

  # ── Radial plot of the preferred (peak) angles ──────────────────────────────
  # Each significant participant is a point on the circle at their peak angle.
  # The black arrow is the circular mean direction; its length is the resultant
  # R (0 = angles spread evenly, 1 = perfectly aligned).
  ang_rad  <- peak_pts$theta_deg * pi / 180
  mean_dir <- atan2(mean(sin(ang_rad)), mean(cos(ang_rad)))
  R_bar    <- sqrt(mean(sin(ang_rad))^2 + mean(cos(ang_rad))^2)

  p_radial <- ggplot(peak_pts, aes(x = theta_deg, y = 1, colour = ParticipantID)) +
    geom_segment(aes(xend = theta_deg, yend = 0), alpha = 0.35) +
    geom_point(size = 4) +
    annotate("segment",
             x = mean_dir * 180 / pi, xend = mean_dir * 180 / pi,
             y = 0, yend = R_bar,
             colour = "black", linewidth = 1.3,
             arrow = grid::arrow(length = grid::unit(0.3, "cm"))) +
    scale_colour_grey(start = 0, end = 0.7) +
    coord_polar(theta = "x", start = 0) +
    scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-180, 180), breaks = seq(-180, 135, 45)) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1.15), breaks = NULL) +
    labs(
      title    = "Preferred (peak) phase angles",
      subtitle = paste0("Black arrow = mean direction (", round(mean_dir * 180 / pi),
                        "\u00b0), resultant R = ", round(R_bar, 2)),
      x        = NULL, y = NULL, colour = "Participant"
    )

  print(p_radial)
}
```
